senators.name: Patty Murray
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from Washington State University. Career as teacher, state legislator, and senator. President Pro Tempore of the Senate. Former Chair of Senate Appropriations Committee and HELP Committee. Decades of deep fiscal and healthcare policy expertise. Known as an effective legislator but approach is more pragmatic-political than analytically rigorous.
experience: President Pro Tempore of the Senate

senators.name: Maria Cantwell
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Miami University (Ohio). Career as technology executive — VP of marketing at RealNetworks (streaming media pioneer). Deep expertise in technology, commerce, and telecommunications policy. As former Chair of the Senate Commerce Committee, led major technology and aviation legislation. One of the few senators with hands-on tech industry executive experience. Applies technology sector knowledge to policy.

WA-01.name: Suzan DelBene
WA-01.nerdReason: B.A. from Reed College and M.B.A. from University of Washington. Career as technology executive at Microsoft (VP of mobile and internet). Chair of the New Democrat Coalition. Reed College provides rigorous analytical liberal arts education. Microsoft VP experience demonstrates deep technology industry expertise. Applies tech sector knowledge to innovation and digital policy.

WA-02.name: Rick Larsen
WA-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from Pacific Lutheran University and M.P.A. from University of Minnesota. Career in public affairs and county council. MPA provides policy analysis training. Deep expertise in transportation and aviation policy. Solid but approach not distinctly analytical beyond standard legislative work.

WA-03.name: Marie Gluesenkamp Perez
WA-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Reed College. Career as auto repair shop owner. Reed College provides exceptionally rigorous academic training (mandatory senior thesis). Blue-collar small business background brings practical perspective. However, legislative approach is more experiential than analytically driven.

WA-04.name: Dan Newhouse
WA-04.notNerdReason: B.S. from Washington State University. Career as farmer and director of Washington State Department of Agriculture. Agricultural background. No distinctly analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

WA-05.name: Michael Baumgartner
WA-05.nerdReason: B.A. from Washington State University, M.A. from University of Washington, and M.B.A. from Harvard Business School. Career as U.S. diplomat (posted to Iraq and Afghanistan), state senator, and county treasurer. Triple-degreed including Harvard MBA. Diplomatic and intelligence experience in conflict zones requires analytical capability. Deep policy expertise across foreign affairs, finance, and economic development.

WA-06.name: Emily Randall
WA-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Washington. Career as state senator and healthcare policy advocate. Policy experience in healthcare and education but approach not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.

WA-07.name: Pramila Jayapal
WA-07.notNerdReason: B.A. from Georgetown University. Career as immigration rights activist, nonprofit executive, and state senator. Chair of the Congressional Progressive Caucus. Deep policy engagement on immigration, healthcare, and economic justice. Strong advocacy but approach driven by activism rather than analytical methodology.

WA-08.name: Kim Schrier
WA-08.nerdReason: B.A. from University of California, Berkeley and M.D. from UC Davis School of Medicine. Practicing pediatrician. Only female physician in Congress during her initial term. Medical training provides rigorous scientific methodology. Applies clinical expertise directly to healthcare, diabetes, and children's health policy. Berkeley + UC Davis medical degree represents strong STEM analytical foundation.
education: M.D., UC Davis School of Medicine

WA-09.name: Adam Smith
WA-09.nerdReason: B.A. from Fordham University and J.D. from University of Washington School of Law. Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee — overseeing the most complex defense policy in Congress. Career as prosecutor and state senator. Deep defense, intelligence, and foreign affairs policy expertise built over decades. Analytical approach to military strategy and defense budgeting.
education: J.D., University of Washington School of Law
experience: Ranking Member, House Armed Services Committee

WA-10.name: Marilyn Strickland
WA-10.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Washington and M.B.A. from Clark Atlanta University. Career as mayor of Tacoma and CEO of the Seattle Metropolitan Chamber of Commerce. MBA provides business analytical training. Executive governance experience but approach not distinctly evidence-based or research-driven.
experience: CEO, Seattle Metropolitan Chamber of Commerce
First African American and Korean American elected to Congress from Washington
